excerpt: this fear derives from the student's dependence upon his mentor for the nourishment of his intellectual form
source_location_hint: discussions on tawhid and spiritual pedagogy

excerpt: when I was about to say 'Here I am,' I grew fearful: what if Allah were to answer 'You have no one but Me'?
source_location_hint: majlis on fear of God and ʿAlī's devotion

excerpt: Kindness is that you worship Allah as though you see Him; and if you do not see Him, know that He sees you
source_location_hint: chapter on excellence and ihsan in worship

excerpt: One hour of your contemplation surpasses fifty years of your worship
source_location_hint: sermon on the importance of reflection

excerpt: Ask questions, ask questions until people begin to say of you that you are mad
source_location_hint: encouragement of inquiry in seeking knowledge

excerpt: If a person lacks the company of a scholar for forty days, then a traveller enters his heart, and he begins to venture toward the great sins
source_location_hint: section on the necessity of scholarly companionship

excerpt: the beneficiary's question arises from the movement of the Imam; the mentor's answer arises from the grace of the Imam—all returns to the Imam's praise
source_location_hint: discussion of the Imam's role in knowledge transmission

excerpt: Allah makes it obligatory for the world to ask the People of Remembrance, but does not obligate them to answer
source_location_hint: exegesis of Qur'an 21:7 on the Imams

excerpt: when the blood reaches the power of imagination in the brain, it acquires the capacity to absorb the forms and images of all worlds through imagination
source_location_hint: discussion of the faculty of imagination and spiritual transformation

excerpt: Verily, those who deny Our verses and treat them with arrogance—for them the gates of heaven will not be opened, nor will they enter Paradise until the camel passes through the eye of the needle
source_location_hint: exegesis of Qur'an 7:40 on arrogance and rejection
